methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $66M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 7 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

private.name: Foundation Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series A), post-money $3B+ (reported, raise in progress)

data.narrative:
Chef Robotics is a Series A robotics and manufacturing company founded by Rajat Bhageria that has raised $65.6M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of 9 private firms and 1 public company operating in the same sector at comparable or adjacent stages. Lead private peers include Foundation Robotics, 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, and Locus Robotics, with valuations ranging from $300M to $10.0B.

Two triangulation methods apply. Stage-progression analysis uses the $66M total raised as a proxy baseline and applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical for Series A advancement, yielding $131M. Peer-median methodology anchors to the median valuation of seven same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables at $1.6B, with a range spanning $300M to $10.0B. This wide peer dispersion reflects material heterogeneity in robotics company trajectories and market positioning.

The triangulated analysis produces a low estimate of $112M, a median of $891M, and a high of $1.9B. The median valuation of $891M sits substantially above stage-progression guidance but materially below the peer median, reflecting the significant maturity gap between Chef Robotics' Series A status and the later-stage, more proven cohort. Key uncertainties include the target's relative technical differentiation, go-to-market traction, and unit economics compared to the peer set—factors that would justify placement toward either end of the $112M–$1.9B range.
